title: 7 homemade seasoning mixes - Canada.ca
content:
## 7 homemade seasoning mixes
Estimated reading time: 5 minutes
No endorsement of any products or services is expressed or implied.
You can enhance the flavour of food by seasoning your meals with herbs and spices. Store-bought spice mixes can be costly and often contain a lot of sodium.
Create your own spice mix recipes using simple ingredients.
Tip: When seasoning a dish with a new spice mix, add a little at a time. This way, you can add more, little by little, until your desired taste is achieved.
- Italian-style seasoning
- Mexican-style seasoning
- Mediterranean-style seasoning
- Middle Eastern-style seasoning
- Chinese-style five spice mix
For the 3-ingredient version, mix:
- 10 mL (2 tsp) onion powder
- 15 mL (1 tbsp) dried oregano
- 30 mL (2 tbsp) smoked paprika
For the 6-ingredient version, add:
- 15 mL (1 tbsp) dried rosemary
- 15 mL (1 tbsp) ground coriander
- 5 mL (1 tsp) ground black pepper
You can sprinkle this mixture during cooking or use it to flavour your marinade for foods such as:
For the 3-ingredient version, mix:
- 15 mL (1 tbsp) cinnamon
- 5 mL (1 tsp) ground ginger
For the 5-ingredient version, add:
- 5 mL (1 tsp) allspice
- 5 mL (1 tsp) ground cloves
To add flavour without adding excess sugars, use this mix to season:

## Italian-style seasoning
For the 3-ingredient version, mix:
- 30 mL (2 tbsp) dried basil
- 10 mL (2 tsp) dried thyme
- 30 mL (2 tbsp) dried oregano
For the 6-ingredient version, add:
- 10 mL (2 tsp) garlic powder
- 30 mL (2 tbsp) dried parsley
- 15 mL (1 tbsp) dried marjoram
This mix is a perfect seasoning for:
You can also use it to make a homemade Italian dressing by mixing:
- 85 mL (⅓ cup) olive oil
- 15 mL (1 tbsp) spice mix
- 15 mL (1 tbsp) lemon juice
- 30 mL (2 tbsp) white balsamic vinegar

## Mexican-style seasoning
For the 3-ingredient version, mix:
- 5 mL (1 tsp) dried oregano
- 30 mL (2 tbsp) chili powder
- 15 mL (1 tbsp) ground paprika
For the 5-ingredient version, add:
- 10 mL (2 tsp) ground cumin
- 10 mL (2 tsp) onion powder
This mix will be delicious when used to season:

## Mediterranean-style seasoning
For the 3-ingredient version, mix:
- 15 mL (1 tbsp) dried thyme
- 15 mL (1 tbsp) dried oregano
- 15 mL (1 tbsp) dried marjoram
For the 6-ingredient version, add:
- 10 mL (2 tsp) dried sage
- 10 mL (2 tsp) dried savory
- 10 mL (2 tsp) dried rosemary
This mix is perfect for tomato-based recipes like ratatouille and pasta. You can also use it to prepare salads or wraps.
Add to meat or fish with a dash of lemon juice after cooking for a tangy twist.

## Middle Eastern-style seasoning
For the 3-ingredient version, mix:
- 15 mL (1 tbsp) ground cumin
- 2 mL (½ tsp) ground turmeric
- 15 mL (1 tbsp) ground coriander
For the 6-ingredient version, add:
- 15 mL (1 tbsp) paprika
- 5 mL (1 tsp) ground cinnamon
- 10 mL (2 tsp) ground fennel seed
Use this mix to add flavour to:
Looking for ideas? These spices are delicious in this Mujadarrah (lentils, onions and rice) recipe.

## Chinese-style five spice mix
For the 3-ingredient version, mix:
- 10 mL (2 tsp) ground cinnamon
- 10 mL (2 tsp) ground fennel seed
For the 5-ingredient version, add:
- 10 mL (2 tsp) ground star anise
- 5 mL (1 tsp) ground Szechuan pepper
This mix is often used in Chinese cuisine. It enhances dishes like:
- Asian-inspired soups
You can also add it to cooked brown rice or sprinkle it on pineapple slices for a tasty snack.

- Measure ingredients like a pro
- Healthy eating and the environment
